caseNarrative:
On March 15, 2025, $SHADY (SHADY) on Ethereum experienced a dramatic +142.5% pump on $2.3M in volume — a move that caught the attention of The Snitch's forensic engine.

The investigation identified three suspect wallets that positioned themselves hours before the price explosion. The primary suspect, 0xaaaa...0001, began accumulating $580K worth of SHADY tokens approximately 18.5 hours before the move. This wallet, flagged as DEX-visible and traceable on-chain, demonstrated a timing advantage that is highly correlated with advance knowledge of the impending price action.

Wallet connection analysis revealed that Suspect #1 and Suspect #2 share a common funding source — both wallets trace back to the same origin, suggesting coordinated activity. The profiler comparison further identified 3 shared counterparties between these wallets, reinforcing the connection.

The evidence breakdown paints a compelling picture: timing scored 80/100, volume concentration hit 70/100 with the top 3 wallets controlling 42% of pre-move volume, and wallet connections scored 100/100 due to the same-funding link. The primary suspect realized $1.2M in profit, a staggering +850% return.

With a suspicion score of 82/100, this case warrants further investigation. The combination of precise timing, concentrated volume, and provable wallet connections makes this one of the more notable cases The Snitch has flagged.
keyFindings: Top suspect traded 18.5h before +142.5% pump with $580K volume
Two suspect wallets trace to same funding source
shareableLine: $SHADY: HIGHLY SUSPICIOUS (82/100) — 3 wallets, same funding source, 18h head start
